title: Xanthous Springy Uruguayan Lentil Pilaf
description: A rich and festive Uruguayan dish featuring lentils, miso-marinate, and a unique cedar-plank vessel, this pilaf is perfect for springtime celebrations. It is a satvik-friendly option that combines flavors and textures in a delightful way.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together miso paste and olive oil to create the marinade.
Add the chopped onion and minced garlic to the marinade and stir to combine.
Add the rinsed and drained green lentils to the marinade and stir to coat.
Season the lentil mixture with salt and black pepper.
Place the soaked cedar plank on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Spoon the lentil mixture onto the cedar plank, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
Bake the pilaf for 35 minutes, or until the lentils are tender and the cedar plank is fragrant.
Remove the pilaf from the oven and sprinkle with chopped fresh parsley.
Serve the Xanthous Springy Uruguayan Lentil Pilaf hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
